summary: hardhat-compile-ethers fetches and executes attacker-controlled npm package at load time
details: The package's main module (dist/src/index.js) contains top-level code that base64-decodes the string 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund', spawns it via child_process, and on completion require()s the freshly installed `zyncmap` package (also referenced via a base64-encoded identifier) and immediately invokes its `getPlugin()()` export. This causes arbitrary attacker-controlled code from the `zyncmap` package to execute on the developer/CI machine the moment the hardhat plugin is imported. The base64 obfuscation of both the install command and the module name, combined with variable names chosen to blend in with surrounding ethers helper code copied from hardhat-deploy-ethers, evidences intentional concealment. The package name and description impersonate the legitimate @nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers / hardhat-deploy-ethers plugins to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
